title: cymbopogon martini
intro: Cymbopogon martini, also known as East Indian lemongrass, is a tropical grass native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its long, slender leaves and distinctive citrus scent. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly citral and geraniol. The medicinal properties of Cymbopogon martini have been shown to provide various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and alleviating stress and anxiety. Cymbopogon martini is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which can be consumed or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
